BackstoryDef.title: garrison soldier
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] spent years at a defensive post, drilling with ranged weapons until accuracy became reflex rather than effort. [PAWN_pronoun] was never asked to think much, and mostly obliged. The post was quiet for long stretches, and [PAWN_pronoun] became very good at quiet stretches.

BackstoryDef.title: professional hunter
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] hunted for a living across several territories before settling into it as a proper profession. [PAWN_pronoun] developed patience, a good eye, and an instinctive disregard for anything that couldn't be eaten or sold. Clients found [PAWN_objective] reliable, if terse.

BackstoryDef.title: security contractor
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: Whoever paid got the protection. [PAWN_nameDef] worked armed security for years without strong feelings about which side [PAWN_pronoun] was on. [PAWN_pronoun] got good at reading situations quickly and better at ending them quietly, in that order.

BackstoryDef.title: pest controller
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: Pest populations in enclosed spaces become serious problems faster than most people expect. [PAWN_nameDef] was methodical about preventing that, working through infested areas with a thoroughness that other people found unsettling and [PAWN_pronoun] found meditative.

BackstoryDef.title: sentry operator
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: Long shifts at a watch station left [PAWN_nameDef] with exceptional situational awareness and a poor opinion of surprise. [PAWN_pronoun] learned to read patterns in emptiness -- to distinguish the silences that were normal from the ones that weren't -- and trusted that instinct completely.

BackstoryDef.title: enforcement agent
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] worked order maintenance in a dense settlement for years, learning that most problems could be solved without firing and that the remainder could not, and developing a reliable sense of which was which before things escalated.

BackstoryDef.title: convoy escort
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] guarded supply lines through contested territory for years. [PAWN_pronoun] fired more rounds than most soldiers, attended fewer funerals, and attributed this to preparation rather than luck. The distinction mattered to [PAWN_objective].

BackstoryDef.title: bounty hunter
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] tracked and apprehended people for money. [PAWN_pronoun] was good at finding them and better at bringing them in, and had developed specific opinions about the ones who made the second part more difficult than it needed to be.

BackstoryDef.title: armed medic
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] patched people up under fire. [PAWN_pronoun] carried a weapon out of necessity and used it more than [PAWN_pronoun] would have preferred, but the triage decisions were what [PAWN_pronoun] found genuinely difficult. The weapon was just physics.

BackstoryDef.title: competition marksman
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] competed in precision shooting for years before deciding that the prizes weren't worth the travel. [PAWN_pronoun] won often enough to be remembered at most circuits. The discipline and the stillness required for the work stayed long after [PAWN_pronoun] stopped competing.

BackstoryDef.title: tunnel fighter
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] fought in close quarters where ranged weapons were a liability and distance was a luxury neither side had. [PAWN_pronoun] became very good at the alternative. Narrow spaces and poor visibility suited [PAWN_possessive] approach in ways that open ground didn't.

BackstoryDef.title: close protection
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] kept people alive at close range for a living. [PAWN_pronoun] was paid to absorb attention and redirect it, and had the scars that demonstrated the method worked. The principals [PAWN_pronoun] protected rarely knew how close things had gotten.

BackstoryDef.title: enforcement brawler
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] handled physical disputes in a dense settlement. [PAWN_pronoun] learned early that a short confrontation was better than a long one, and that most disputes weren't really about what they appeared to be about. [PAWN_pronoun] ended the confrontation either way.

BackstoryDef.title: competitive fighter
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] fought in organized competitions for years. [PAWN_pronoun] was technically accomplished and personally difficult, and the other competitors eventually stopped wanting to share practice space with [PAWN_objective]. [PAWN_pronoun] didn't take it personally.

BackstoryDef.title: heavy laborer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] did physical work for most of [PAWN_possessive] adult life. [PAWN_pronoun] was not a fighter by training but by accumulated experience in environments where the distinction between a worker and someone who could handle themselves didn't hold for long.

BackstoryDef.title: hired fighter
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] fought for whoever was paying when [PAWN_pronoun] needed money and stopped when [PAWN_pronoun] didn't. [PAWN_pronoun] never had strong feelings about which side [PAWN_pronoun] was on. This made [PAWN_objective] easy to hire and difficult to trust, and [PAWN_pronoun] understood both.

BackstoryDef.title: detention officer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] worked at a detention facility for several years, where [PAWN_pronoun] broke up more fights than [PAWN_pronoun] started but started a few. The environment developed a particular kind of alertness and a tolerance for unresolved tension that persisted afterward.

BackstoryDef.title: riot officer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: Civil disorder has a predictable shape once you have seen enough of it. [PAWN_nameDef] had seen enough of it. [PAWN_pronoun] was deployed to control unrest on several occasions and got good at reading the point before which intervention helped and after which it didn't.

BackstoryDef.title: freight worker
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] moved cargo in environments where the cargo sometimes pushed back -- live animals, unstable materials, containers that hadn't been properly secured. [PAWN_pronoun] developed the hands and the reflexes and a good memory for which categories of load required which precautions.

BackstoryDef.title: arena veteran
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] fought in organized exhibition bouts for a crowd that wanted a spectacle. [PAWN_pronoun] gave them one, developed a precise technical skill that most spectators couldn't see, and retired with most of [PAWN_possessive] teeth and all of [PAWN_possessive] hearing.

BackstoryDef.title: structural engineer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] designed and maintained load-bearing systems. [PAWN_pronoun] could look at a wall and tell you exactly when it would fail, exactly why, and exactly what would need to be done to prevent it. Most people trusted structures without examining them. [PAWN_nameDef] examined everything.

BackstoryDef.title: enclosed habitat builder
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] constructed enclosed living spaces in environments that made the work difficult -- extreme temperatures, low oxygen, unstable ground. [PAWN_pronoun] understood that a bad seal killed people quietly and without warning, and took every join personally.

BackstoryDef.title: infrastructure tech
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] maintained the systems that kept enclosed settlements functional -- power, atmosphere, water, waste. [PAWN_pronoun] was called at inconvenient hours and fixed things before they became emergencies. The work was invisible when it went right and impossible to ignore when it didn't.

BackstoryDef.title: demolition expert
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] brought structures down on schedule and within budget. [PAWN_pronoun] had a precise relationship with controlled destruction and a complicated one with everything else. Demolition requires more patience in the planning than most people expected from someone in that line of work.

BackstoryDef.title: pressure sealer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] specialized in atmospheric integrity work -- sealing breaches, testing tolerances, and verifying joins under conditions that made the consequences of error immediate. [PAWN_pronoun] had strong opinions about people who checked things once and kept them largely to [PAWN_objective]self.

BackstoryDef.title: civil contractor
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] built what clients required and charged what the market allowed. [PAWN_pronoun] was competent, punctual, and largely unconcerned with what the buildings were eventually used for. [PAWN_pronoun] had opinions about quality and none at all about purpose.

BackstoryDef.title: tunnel engineer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] carved passages through rock for a living. [PAWN_pronoun] knew the difference between a tunnel that would hold indefinitely and one that would hold long enough to give notice before it didn't, and knew which clients could be told which kind they were getting.

BackstoryDef.title: emergency repair tech
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] responded to structural failures under pressure, arriving at scenes where something had already gone wrong and working to prevent the secondary failures that followed the first. [PAWN_pronoun] was calm in the way that comes from having seen the alternative.

BackstoryDef.title: welding specialist
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] joined materials for a living. [PAWN_pronoun] had steady hands, good eyes, and no interest in conversations during work. A good weld is invisible -- you cannot see the join when it is done correctly -- and [PAWN_pronoun] had not left a visible join in years.

BackstoryDef.title: construction foreman
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] ran crews on large build sites for most of [PAWN_possessive] working life. [PAWN_pronoun] was responsible for what got built, how fast, and how many people went home at the end of each day. Foremen live in the gap between the plans and what the plans don't account for.

BackstoryDef.title: deep miner
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] worked extraction in the lower levels where most people didn't go and where the ones who did developed their own habits and their own understanding of how rock behaves. [PAWN_pronoun] learned it well enough to read it and respected it enough to be careful when the reading was ambiguous.

BackstoryDef.title: survey geologist
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] assessed rock formations for extraction viability, reading the mineral record in the stone before the first drill ran. [PAWN_pronoun] could tell you what was in a wall and roughly what it would take to get it out, and was rarely wrong about either.

BackstoryDef.title: extraction worker
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] ran extraction equipment in industrial operations. [PAWN_pronoun] kept the machines running and was there every day, which was all that was asked and more than was usual. The equipment had moods that [PAWN_pronoun] had learned to anticipate before they became breakdowns.

BackstoryDef.title: blast technician
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] placed charges for a living and was meticulous about it, because the alternative was educational in the worst possible way. The meticulous nature extended to everything [PAWN_pronoun] did and made [PAWN_objective] slow and thorough in ways that most employers eventually appreciated.

BackstoryDef.title: tunnel cutter
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] cut new passages through stone, working fast and reading rock formation in real time. [PAWN_pronoun] had an unshakeable preference for closed spaces over open ones that [PAWN_pronoun] made no effort to explain to people who couldn't understand it anyway.

BackstoryDef.title: ore processor
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] sorted and processed raw ore through multiple stages of refinement. [PAWN_pronoun] knew the material better than the engineers who oversaw the operation, told them so regularly, and was eventually proven right enough times that they stopped arguing about it.

BackstoryDef.title: shaft sinker
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] dug vertical shafts in difficult ground conditions. The work was dangerous, poorly paid, and [PAWN_pronoun] was good at it, which kept [PAWN_objective] employed in a line of work that consistently paid less than the risk justified. [PAWN_pronoun] knew the math and did it anyway.

BackstoryDef.title: resource scout
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] located viable deposits ahead of extraction crews, spending more time alone in difficult terrain than with other people. [PAWN_pronoun] preferred it that way. Scout work is reading the landscape for what it conceals rather than what it shows, and [PAWN_pronoun] was patient at both.

BackstoryDef.title: mine foreman
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] ran extraction crews, keeping people productive and keeping them safe, which required knowing the difference between acceptable risk and the kind that generated paperwork. [PAWN_pronoun] knew both categories well and could usually identify which one was developing before it did.

BackstoryDef.title: salvage miner
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] extracted useful material from collapsed or abandoned sites. [PAWN_pronoun] was good at finding what remained, fast at assessing what was structurally viable, and decisive about how much of a recovery was worth the risk of completing it.

BackstoryDef.title: community cook
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] fed large numbers of people on limited supplies for years, developing creative substitutions and strong opinions about waste that [PAWN_pronoun] applied with equal force to ingredients and to the people who mishandled them.

BackstoryDef.title: ration manager
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] managed food distribution during a period of extended scarcity. [PAWN_pronoun] kept people fed, kept the books accurate, and lost sleep over the gap between the two, which was never as small as the books suggested.

BackstoryDef.title: fermentation specialist
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] produced preserved and fermented goods professionally. [PAWN_pronoun] understood the process at a level that most people never needed and could identify exactly what had gone wrong with any batch, usually from smell alone before opening it.

BackstoryDef.title: galley chief
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: Running a communal kitchen at scale is an exercise in controlled chaos under time pressure. [PAWN_nameDef] was demanding, organized, and consistently produced results that the available ingredients didn't deserve. [PAWN_pronoun] was not comfortable to work under and the food was worth it.

BackstoryDef.title: industrial cook
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] prepared meals for large work crews on industrial sites, learning speed, scale, and how much the performance of physical laborers depended on what they ate and when. A tired crew eating poorly produced less work and more accidents, and [PAWN_pronoun] tracked both.

BackstoryDef.title: nutrition specialist
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] managed dietary requirements for a population with limited food variety, keeping people healthy with what was available. This required knowing what could substitute for what and what couldn't, and maintaining the discipline to enforce the distinction under pressure.

BackstoryDef.title: food preserver
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] extended the shelf life of food supplies through preservation and processing techniques. [PAWN_pronoun] understood spoilage at a practical level and worked against it with the same methodical attention that other people brought to more dramatic problems.

BackstoryDef.title: dietary cook
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] prepared food for people with medical requirements -- specific allergies, dietary restrictions, recovery protocols. [PAWN_pronoun] learned enough pharmacology to do the job properly and enough cooking to make the result worth eating, which were not always compatible goals.

BackstoryDef.title: foraging specialist
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] sourced food from the environment and prepared it for consumption, developing a working knowledge of what was edible, what was borderline, and what would kill you faster than starvation while tasting considerably better.

BackstoryDef.title: brew master
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] produced fermented goods at a level that attracted attention from people who knew what they were looking at. [PAWN_pronoun] had precise opinions about process and the social skills of someone who had never needed anyone else's opinion and saw no reason to start needing it now.

BackstoryDef.title: hydroponics tech
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] managed enclosed growing systems for years, developing an understanding of plant requirements that went well past the operational manual. [PAWN_pronoun] knew what each variety needed before it showed symptoms and was rarely caught by something [PAWN_pronoun] hadn't already anticipated.

BackstoryDef.title: botanical researcher
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] studied plant biology in a formal research context, accumulating detailed knowledge that most practical applications never required. [PAWN_pronoun] used it anyway, on the grounds that knowing more than necessary was better than finding out where the boundary was.

BackstoryDef.title: crop manager
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] ran agricultural operations at scale, managing yield, rotation, and the particular difficulty of getting biological systems to cooperate with human scheduling. Plants don't respect calendars, and [PAWN_pronoun] had developed a productive working relationship with that fact.

BackstoryDef.title: seed curator
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] maintained genetic archives of plant material, keeping samples viable and records accurate over years of careful work. [PAWN_pronoun] treated the work as important, which it was, and as urgent, which it occasionally became when something went wrong with the storage.

BackstoryDef.title: medicinal herbalist
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] cultivated and prepared plant-based remedies, developing a working pharmacology through practice and careful observation. [PAWN_pronoun] understood the method's limitations clearly enough to know when to refer out and wasn't ideologically attached to either option.

BackstoryDef.title: garden keeper
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] maintained growing spaces in an enclosed settlement, doing the work carefully, keeping detailed records, and developing a temperamental unsuitability for interruptions that the plants didn't cause and the people did.

BackstoryDef.title: propagation specialist
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] bred and cultivated plant varieties suited to controlled environments, selecting for the traits that made them viable under artificial conditions. [PAWN_pronoun] had a long view of things and the patience it required. Propagation is work that rewards the patient and punishes the hasty.

BackstoryDef.title: forest worker
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] managed a controlled woodland -- culling, replanting, monitoring for disease, maintaining the balance between growth and removal. [PAWN_pronoun] worked mostly alone and was comfortable with that. Managed woodland is not wild and not a farm, and [PAWN_pronoun] understood what it was.

BackstoryDef.title: agro technician
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] ran the technical side of agricultural operations -- equipment, environmental controls, irrigation systems. [PAWN_pronoun] could diagnose a failing crop before it showed visible symptoms by reading the mechanical data before the biological data confirmed it.

BackstoryDef.title: wild collector
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] gathered plant material from unmanaged environments for research and supply purposes. [PAWN_pronoun] knew which areas were safe, which were safe enough, and which were neither -- and had a clear policy about the third category that [PAWN_pronoun] applied consistently.

BackstoryDef.title: animal handler
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] managed working animals in a large facility, developing a calm around them that [PAWN_pronoun] was not always able to produce around people. Animals respond to calmness reliably. [PAWN_pronoun] found this a more satisfying basis for a professional relationship than most of the alternatives.

BackstoryDef.title: veterinary practitioner
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] treated animals in a working medical capacity, developing clinical knowledge and the steady hands that the work required. [PAWN_pronoun] consistently had less authority than [PAWN_pronoun] needed to do the job properly and did it anyway.

BackstoryDef.title: wildlife manager
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] managed animal populations in controlled and semi-controlled environments, preventing the problems that emerged when those populations went unmanaged. [PAWN_pronoun] prevented more disasters than anyone knew about, which is how successful wildlife management looks from the outside.

BackstoryDef.title: breeding manager
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] ran a breeding program, selecting for useful traits and managing the outcomes over multiple generations. [PAWN_pronoun] had a methodical mind and accumulated more practical genetics knowledge than most formal education programs covered.

BackstoryDef.title: tracking specialist
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] located and followed animals through difficult terrain for research, population management, and occasional elimination. [PAWN_pronoun] was very good at the quiet parts -- the patience, the reading of terrain, the avoidance of unnecessary noise.

BackstoryDef.title: animal trainer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] trained animals for protective work, developing an understanding of animal cognition that the work required and the patience that made it possible. Animal training is a negotiation. [PAWN_pronoun] was a good negotiator with considerably less language than [PAWN_pronoun] used elsewhere.

BackstoryDef.title: pest manager
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] controlled animal populations that caused damage in enclosed settlements. [PAWN_pronoun] was thorough, discreet, and never made the same mistake twice. In enclosed environments, pest populations can reach serious levels faster than most administrators wanted to believe.

BackstoryDef.title: draft keeper
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: Working draft animals require handlers who understand their physical limits and their behavioral moods in equal measure. [PAWN_nameDef] developed both over years of daily contact and understood exactly how far either could be pushed without producing a result nobody wanted.

BackstoryDef.title: animal researcher
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] studied animal behavior in a research capacity, maintaining careful data and careful handling protocols over extended observation periods. [PAWN_pronoun] was considerably less careful about [PAWN_possessive] relationships with the other researchers, who noticed.

BackstoryDef.title: herd manager
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] ran large-scale animal husbandry, making decisions about large numbers of living things every day and developing a durable comfort with that responsibility. Herd management requires thinking about the group before the individual, which takes practice.

BackstoryDef.title: weaponsmith
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] made and maintained weapons for a living. [PAWN_pronoun] knew the tolerances intimately and tested [PAWN_possessive] work personally before it went out, which kept the quality high and gave [PAWN_objective] a permanent preference for things that were built to a standard.

BackstoryDef.title: machinist
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] worked metal at precision tolerances for most of [PAWN_possessive] career. [PAWN_pronoun] was methodical, kept a clean workspace, and had no tolerance for approximation. Precision machining punishes carelessness consistently and [PAWN_pronoun] had arranged [PAWN_possessive] life so it punished other people's carelessness instead.

BackstoryDef.title: armor fabricator
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] produced protective equipment for working and fighting environments. [PAWN_pronoun] understood what the armor was supposed to stop and built accordingly, aware that armor which fails costs the wearer considerably more than armor which works costs the maker.

BackstoryDef.title: generalist crafter
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] made whatever was needed across a wide range of materials and methods. [PAWN_pronoun] had worked enough different trades to be competent with most and expert with some, and knew which was which without needing to be asked.

BackstoryDef.title: textile worker
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] produced cloth and garments in a working facility. [PAWN_pronoun] was fast, accurate, and knew the difference between good work and acceptable work. [PAWN_pronoun] always produced the former and could identify the latter in someone else's output at a glance.

BackstoryDef.title: electronics technician
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] built and repaired electronic components at a level that most people in [PAWN_possessive] vicinity didn't understand. [PAWN_pronoun] explained things in terms that most people didn't follow and had stopped adjusting the explanations years ago. The work spoke for itself.

BackstoryDef.title: repair specialist
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] fixed things other people had given up on, applying a diagnostic mind and the patience to use it to anything that had stopped working. The satisfaction was in the restoration -- the moment a thing that had been broken was working again. [PAWN_pronoun] never stopped finding it satisfying.

BackstoryDef.title: chemical synthesizer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] produced chemical compounds in a working facility. [PAWN_pronoun] was precise and careful, acutely aware that the margin between useful and dangerous was a matter of measurement rather than intention. [PAWN_pronoun] kept meticulous records and reviewed them before every production run.

BackstoryDef.title: salvage engineer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] recovered and repurposed materials from abandoned or damaged equipment. [PAWN_pronoun] saw potential in things others had written off, and had a particular talent for identifying which abandoned systems could be made functional again and which could only contribute parts toward something else.

BackstoryDef.title: master crafter
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] spent decades developing craft skills to a level that most practitioners in [PAWN_possessive] field never reached. [PAWN_pronoun] was known for the quality of [PAWN_possessive] work and for an intolerance for inferior results that [PAWN_pronoun] applied to [PAWN_objective]self as often as to anyone else.

BackstoryDef.title: settlement artist
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] made art for and about enclosed communities for most of [PAWN_possessive] working life. [PAWN_pronoun] had a specific eye for what mattered in those environments -- the light, the density, the particular quality of time in a sealed space -- and the skill to render it accurately.

BackstoryDef.title: information designer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] produced visual and informational materials for public consumption. [PAWN_pronoun] was good at making things look more true than they were and occasionally, by accident or by persistence, made them actually true. Design shapes belief before belief has time to examine the evidence.

BackstoryDef.title: documentary artist
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] documented communities and events in artistic form for years, developing the habit of watching carefully and the skill of rendering what [PAWN_pronoun] saw. Documentary work requires the artist to be present without becoming part of what [PAWN_pronoun] is documenting. [PAWN_nameDef] managed this most of the time.

BackstoryDef.title: functional designer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] designed objects intended to be both used and looked at. [PAWN_pronoun] could not produce something purely functional and considered this an instinct rather than a constraint. The argument that form should follow function struck [PAWN_objective] as missing the point of having both.

BackstoryDef.title: mural painter
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] painted large-scale works for public spaces, developing a comfort at height and on scaffolding that most painters didn't share. Public art has to work from distances its creator never occupies while it's being made. [PAWN_pronoun] had learned to plan for that absence.

BackstoryDef.title: sculptor
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] worked in three dimensions, producing pieces that ranged from commemorative to incomprehensible. [PAWN_pronoun] was proud of both categories and unwilling to rank them. Sculpture occupies space rather than depicting it, which [PAWN_pronoun] considered a fundamentally more honest relationship between art and audience.

BackstoryDef.title: chronicler
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] recorded history and current events in written form. [PAWN_pronoun] was accurate, readable, and uncomfortable to have around if you had done something worth putting in the record. Chroniclers are the people who ensure that things happened -- and that the people responsible for them are attached to what they did.

BackstoryDef.title: performing artist
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] performed for live audiences and learned, over years of practice, to read a room, hold attention, and recover from anything that went wrong in front of people. Performance failure is public in a way that other failure isn't. [PAWN_pronoun] had failed publicly enough to stop fearing it.

BackstoryDef.title: technical illustrator
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] produced technical drawings and diagrams for engineering and construction applications. [PAWN_pronoun] worked at the intersection of accuracy and clarity, understanding that a technically correct diagram that nobody could interpret was a diagram that had failed its only purpose.

BackstoryDef.title: trade artisan
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] produced handcrafted goods at a quality above market standard. [PAWN_pronoun] worked slowly, charged accordingly, and refused to hurry. The refusal wasn't stubbornness -- it was an accurate understanding of what haste did to the work and a preference for not finding out again.

BackstoryDef.title: field surgeon
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] operated in difficult conditions with limited supplies and limited time. [PAWN_pronoun] made decisions fast and lived with them afterward, which is the only way to do field surgery and not be destroyed by it. The conditions stripped medicine down to what was essential.

BackstoryDef.title: community physician
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] ran a small practice in a dense settlement for years, treating everything that came through the door. [PAWN_pronoun] knew [PAWN_possessive] patients and their histories and used both, because a physician who knows their patients knows things those patients didn't tell them.

BackstoryDef.title: pharmacy manager
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] managed pharmaceutical production and distribution. [PAWN_pronoun] understood what each compound did, what it did in combination, what happened when a dose was wrong, and what happened when it ran out. [PAWN_pronoun] kept careful records of all four categories.

BackstoryDef.title: trauma specialist
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] treated acute injuries in high-pressure environments, developing a speed and decisiveness that the work required and a calm that the work trained into [PAWN_objective] over time. The calm was learned rather than natural, which [PAWN_pronoun] considered the more reliable kind.

BackstoryDef.title: research medic
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] worked in a medical research context, developing treatments and testing procedures with the rigor and patience the field required. [PAWN_pronoun] was genuinely curious about the limits of what the body could survive, which is either a useful quality in a medical researcher or a troubling one.

BackstoryDef.title: surgical assistant
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] assisted in surgical procedures over many years and absorbed expertise through proximity -- watching, anticipating, handling instruments before they were asked for. [PAWN_pronoun] eventually performed the same procedures independently and, by most accounts, did them better.

BackstoryDef.title: epidemic response worker
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] worked outbreak response in dense settlements, developing a specific understanding of how illness moved through enclosed populations and what interventions actually slowed it. The work required acting on incomplete information under pressure, and [PAWN_pronoun] was good at both.

BackstoryDef.title: herbal practitioner
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] practiced medicine with plant-derived compounds. [PAWN_pronoun] knew the method's limitations clearly and supplemented with other approaches where necessary, without ideology about either. The ideology, [PAWN_pronoun] had observed, was usually the first thing that got patients hurt.

BackstoryDef.title: medical training officer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] trained others in medical procedures. [PAWN_pronoun] was clear, demanding, and considerably better at teaching than at tolerating the mistakes that teaching inevitably produced. The impatience was productive on balance, which [PAWN_pronoun] used to justify it.

BackstoryDef.title: psychiatric worker
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] supported people through severe mental health crises. [PAWN_pronoun] was trained not to take what [PAWN_pronoun] heard home with [PAWN_objective], and mostly succeeded. The acute cases were manageable. The chronic ones, and the ones [PAWN_pronoun] couldn't reach at all, were harder.

BackstoryDef.title: community leader
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] was elected to represent a community in governance, then re-elected, then re-elected again. [PAWN_pronoun] is tired. Representation requires translating what people want into what is achievable and then explaining the gap in terms that don't make both sides angry simultaneously.

BackstoryDef.title: negotiation specialist
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] resolved disputes professionally. [PAWN_pronoun] was good at finding the agreement that both parties could accept and staying in the room until they found it. Most failed negotiations, [PAWN_pronoun] had observed, failed because one party left before the agreement was reachable.

BackstoryDef.title: trade envoy
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] managed trade relationships between communities for years. [PAWN_pronoun] traveled, negotiated, and developed a precise sense of what different people valued and why, which [PAWN_pronoun] found useful in contexts well beyond trade negotiations.

BackstoryDef.title: public advocate
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] argued for the interests of people who couldn't argue effectively for themselves. [PAWN_pronoun] was persistent in the way that made administrators check whether the door was properly secured. Advocacy is repetition with increasing specificity until someone with authority finally listens.

BackstoryDef.title: settlement counselor
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] provided emotional and practical support in high-stress community environments. [PAWN_pronoun] heard a great deal over the years and shared very little. The combination of containment and genuine presence required to do the work well is harder to develop than most training programs suggested.

BackstoryDef.title: information broker
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] traded in knowledge, connecting people who had information with people who needed it. [PAWN_pronoun] maintained networks carefully, cultivated relationships strategically, and kept [PAWN_possessive] own affairs entirely separate from both. The value of a broker is their neutrality.

BackstoryDef.title: public speaker
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] communicated to large groups professionally. [PAWN_pronoun] learned what people wanted to hear and the considerably harder skill of getting them to listen to what they needed to hear instead, without losing the room in the process.

BackstoryDef.title: recruitment officer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] persuaded people to join organizations. [PAWN_pronoun] was good at identifying what someone actually wanted and finding the version of the offer that contained it. [PAWN_pronoun] was better at this than [PAWN_pronoun] was comfortable admitting, which is the correct relationship to have with that skill.

BackstoryDef.title: market operator
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] ran a trading operation in a dense settlement market. [PAWN_pronoun] knew what things were worth, what people would pay for them, and the difference between those two numbers, which is where [PAWN_pronoun] made a living.

BackstoryDef.title: crisis mediator
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] de-escalated volatile situations between groups for a living. [PAWN_pronoun] walked into rooms where things were about to go badly and left having made them not. Crisis mediation is speed and accuracy applied to human psychology under conditions that punish both errors and hesitation.

BackstoryDef.title: systems analyst
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] analyzed complex systems for failure points and optimization opportunities. [PAWN_pronoun] was right often enough to be valued and direct enough to be avoided. Systems analysts are most useful when their findings are uncomfortable, and [PAWN_nameDef]'s findings usually were.

BackstoryDef.title: applied researcher
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] conducted research with practical applications, moving between disciplines without ceremony when the problem required it. [PAWN_pronoun] produced results at a rate that made specialists in adjacent fields uncomfortable and [PAWN_pronoun] noticed their discomfort with interest.

BackstoryDef.title: data manager
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] organized, maintained, and interpreted large information systems over years of careful work. [PAWN_pronoun] knew where everything was, what it meant, and who wasn't supposed to see it. There are sections of the archive that only [PAWN_pronoun] can reliably navigate.

BackstoryDef.title: strategic planner
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] developed operational plans for complex problems. [PAWN_pronoun] thought several steps ahead as a matter of habit and was occasionally surprised when other people didn't. Planning well requires imagining failure modes before they occur. [PAWN_pronoun] imagined a great many.

BackstoryDef.title: field repair engineer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] solved engineering problems in the field without the benefit of the right tools or enough time. [PAWN_pronoun] developed a practical relationship with improvisation over years of making available resources solve necessary problems, and became good at it in ways that classrooms couldn't have produced.

BackstoryDef.title: technical writer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] documented procedures and systems in writing. [PAWN_pronoun] made complex things understandable, which was harder than making them complex and required knowing the subject more thoroughly than the people who had originally developed it.

BackstoryDef.title: navigation specialist
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] planned and executed routes through difficult environments for years. [PAWN_pronoun] was rarely lost and found the concept personally offensive. Navigation under uncertainty requires constant revision of the current estimate, and [PAWN_pronoun] revised constantly and without ego.

BackstoryDef.title: practical inventor
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] developed functional devices for specific problems. [PAWN_pronoun] was not interested in invention for its own sake -- only in whether the thing worked -- and this constraint made [PAWN_objective] a better inventor than most people who started with broader goals.

BackstoryDef.title: systems inspector
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] assessed operations, identified problems, and documented them with precision. [PAWN_pronoun] was not popular and considered that a professional asset rather than a personal failing. Audit findings that make everyone comfortable are not audit findings.

BackstoryDef.title: academic instructor
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] taught in a formal educational context for years. [PAWN_pronoun] knew the subject thoroughly, explained it with clarity, and had no patience for the third type of student -- the one who asked questions designed to avoid the answer rather than find it.

BackstoryDef.title: colony settler
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] helped establish a new settlement from its beginning, back when everything that existed was what people had carried with them. [PAWN_pronoun] could build, grow, dig, and repair, and learned through necessity which order those skills were needed in and how fast that order could change.

BackstoryDef.title: drifter
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] never stayed anywhere long enough to become expert at any single thing. [PAWN_pronoun] accumulated a functional knowledge of many trades and a deep knowledge of none, which was either a reasonable response to uncertainty or a way of not committing to anything. Possibly both.

BackstoryDef.title: castaway
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] survived alone in a difficult environment for long enough to stop counting the days. [PAWN_possessive] survival skills sharpened to the point of reflex. [PAWN_possessive] social ones did not, and the recovery was slower than [PAWN_pronoun] expected and less complete than [PAWN_pronoun] would have liked.

BackstoryDef.title: industrial worker
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] did the physical work that kept an industrial operation running. [PAWN_pronoun] was not asked to think and developed the particular competence of people who are not asked to think -- a precise, embodied understanding of what the work required that no one who only supervised it ever quite possessed.

BackstoryDef.title: escaped laborer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: The work was involuntary and the conditions made escape a reasonable priority. [PAWN_nameDef] tunneled out using improvised tools and a patience [PAWN_pronoun] didn't know [PAWN_pronoun] had until the work required it. The tunnel took months and [PAWN_pronoun] would do it again.

BackstoryDef.title: ascetic practitioner
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: After taking a vow of silence, [PAWN_nameDef] joined a contemplative community to spend [PAWN_possessive] days in quiet practice. [PAWN_pronoun] found satisfaction in growing food and preparing it and in the particular quality of a life structured by ritual rather than by urgency. [PAWN_pronoun] missed it, sometimes.

BackstoryDef.title: household manager
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: While [PAWN_possessive] spouse worked, [PAWN_nameDef] ran the household -- cooking, maintaining, managing the medical needs of the people [PAWN_pronoun] lived with. [PAWN_pronoun] did not consider any of this to be minor work, and was correct.

BackstoryDef.title: trade caravan leader
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] led a nomadic trading caravan. It was [PAWN_possessive] duty to negotiate trade deals and ensure the safety of [PAWN_possessive] caravan from brigands and highwaymen. [PAWN_pronoun] became a master at managing wares, plotting routes, and striking deals that both sides found acceptable.

BackstoryDef.title: recluse
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: Worn out by the demands of social existence, [PAWN_nameDef] withdrew to a simpler life. [PAWN_pronoun] tended a garden, made what [PAWN_pronoun] needed, and found the company of plants and animals considerably more satisfying than the alternative. [PAWN_pronoun] had not expected to miss people, and mostly didn't.

BackstoryDef.title: former convict
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: Significant time in a detention facility leaves marks. [PAWN_nameDef] was put to work in the kitchens, developed a habit of fighting that the environment encouraged and [PAWN_pronoun] could not fully leave behind after leaving, and emerged with practical skills and a complicated relationship with authority.

BackstoryDef.title: traveling medic
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] moved between settlements providing medical care, treating what was available to treat, improvising where necessary, and leaving before becoming indispensable. The leaving was intentional -- [PAWN_pronoun] had found that staying too long in one place changed the nature of the work.

BackstoryDef.title: disillusioned academic
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: Walking away from a research position takes a specific kind of disillusionment. [PAWN_nameDef] had it. [PAWN_pronoun] retained the analytical habits from [PAWN_possessive] previous life and shed most of the rest, spending the subsequent years doing unstructured work that [PAWN_pronoun] turned out to be better at.

BackstoryDef.title: black market trader
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] operated in the grey areas of settlement commerce, trading in things that people needed and couldn't obtain through official channels. [PAWN_pronoun] kept careful track of what [PAWN_pronoun] owed and to whom, because the ledger was the most important thing and the only thing that couldn't be improvised.

BackstoryDef.title: subsistence cultivator
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] produced food for [PAWN_possessive] own needs and occasionally for others', working a small cultivated area and supplementing it with whatever the surrounding environment offered. [PAWN_pronoun] knew the land, wasted nothing, and asked nothing of anyone. Self-sufficiency as a practice rather than a philosophy.

BackstoryDef.title: general mechanic
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] fixed mechanical systems across many different types. [PAWN_pronoun] was not expert in any single area but competent across most, and had developed the more valuable skill of learning a new system quickly enough that the distinction rarely mattered in practice.

BackstoryDef.title: disaster survivor
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] lived through a serious infrastructure failure. [PAWN_pronoun] provided emergency care, maintained what could be maintained, and contributed to the recovery in whatever way the moment required. [PAWN_pronoun] remembers exactly what it felt like when the lights came back on. The memory is useful.

BackstoryDef.title: con operator
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] never created anything in [PAWN_possessive] life. [PAWN_pronoun] did, however, prove to be a natural at getting others to give [PAWN_objective] what they had created. [PAWN_pronoun] was very good at this and had mixed feelings about the fact.

BackstoryDef.title: armed caravan guard
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] protected goods in transit through difficult territory. [PAWN_pronoun] was professional about it -- got paid, stayed alert, and didn't make the job personal. Not making it personal was the discipline that distinguished professionals from the people who got killed.

BackstoryDef.title: orphaned laborer
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] worked from a young age without the benefit of family or formal education. [PAWN_pronoun] absorbed practical skills through necessity and social ones through observation, developing a self-sufficiency that looked like competence and was indistinguishable from it.

BackstoryDef.title: information agent
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] distributed and shaped narratives on behalf of a governing body. [PAWN_pronoun] was skilled at the work and selective about what [PAWN_pronoun] personally believed of it, which is probably the only way to do it for long without it doing something permanent to you.

BackstoryDef.title: retired soldier
BackstoryDef.baseDesc: [PAWN_nameDef] served long enough that retirement became a medical recommendation rather than a choice. [PAWN_pronoun] left with complete skills and incomplete adjustment. The adjustment was a longer project than the service had been, and [PAWN_pronoun] was still working on it.
